decision: PR head branches are immutable after PR creation and must be replaced with a fresh branch and fresh PR for any further change.
rationale: The operator requires that GitHub PRs never be updated after creation; local enforcement needs to block accidental pushes before they mutate a PR branch.
alternatives: Relying on operator memory or review discipline was rejected because it permits accidental branch pushes; GitHub branch protection was not encoded here because local hooks can be installed immediately in this checkout without repository admin changes.

decision: Treat attempted no-op pushes and PR pre-creation as violations of the immutable PR workflow.
rationale: A no-op git push can return without remote mutation, but the operator requirement is stricter than remote diff immutability: once a PR exists, the branch must not be touched or probed through push commands. PRs must also be created only after local validation is complete, not as placeholders while work continues.
alternatives: Allowing no-op pushes was rejected because it trains unsafe behavior and bypasses the intended block path. Creating draft PRs before validation was rejected because it produces PRs that are not review-ready and can invite later updates.
